rows.description: Receipts by Messrs. Biddulph
rows.notes: Very clear, large heading at top of page. No monetary figures on the same line; vertical ruling lines not relevant for this row.

rows.description: Cocks, Ridge & Co. Bankers, London
rows.notes: Clear continuation of the heading on the next line; no amounts present.

rows.description: Jan. Half years Divn. on 5708-17-5 3 pr cent (cut consols - )
rows.notes: Description legible. Pounds (76) and shillings (12) align clearly between first and second ruling lines. Pence column appears blank (no pencilled digits), so interpreted as 0d; this is consistent with the printed total below but pence digit is faint/absent.

rows.description: April - Do. - on 5460-0-9 - 4 pr cent -
rows.notes: Pounds (10) and shillings (18) clearly aligned in the pounds/shillings columns. Pence column is blank or very faint; taken as 0d. Handwriting of 'Do.' and numbers is reasonably clear.

rows.description: July - Do. - 3 pr cent, as above -
rows.notes: Mirrors the January dividend line. Pounds/shillings (76/12) align well in columns; pence column blank and read as 0d. Some small smearing but column alignment supports reading.

rows.description: Oct. - Do. - 4 pr cent, as above -
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings (10/18) align clearly. Pence column is blank/faint; interpreted as 0d consistent with the pattern of the other 'Do.' lines.

rows.description: Exchequer Annuity, a year to July 5 -
rows.notes: The pounds (50) and shillings (1) are legible and align in their columns. The pence digit looks like '10' (two digits) but is somewhat cramped and less clear than other pence readings; chosen as 10d because it yields the clearly-inked subtotal beneath. Marked moderate confidence due to faintness of the pence digits.

Underlined subtotal at right of the top section showing 225/1/10. The digits are written more boldly than some item pence digits; alignment with ruling lines is clear. This total guided the interpretation of the faint Exchequer pence above (see row 7).

rows.description: Recep ta varia
rows.notes: Large centered section heading (appears as 'Recep ta varia' in the handwriting). No monetary figures on the same line; text clear though slightly stylized.
Year marker at left margin. Clear and unambiguous; no amounts on this small left-hand mark.

rows.description: Jan. 7 Of the V. Chancellor, the rent of the 50£ subscribed for (see Jan.26 in last Acct)
rows.notes: Description readable though some words abbreviated. Pounds (33), shillings (6) and pence (8) align with the three ruling lines and are legible; moderate confidence due to slightly cramped pence digit but within valid range.

rows.description: — 19 Dr. Shorthidge's Benefaction to Michl. 1801
rows.notes: Large, clear figure '110' in pounds column. No shillings/pence recorded (both blank) which is common for whole-pound entries; handwriting of description legible.

rows.description: Dec. 19 Long Wittenham Rent to Michl. last
rows.notes: Large, bold '550' in pounds column clearly aligned. No shillings/pence indicated. Description readable ('Long Wittenham').

rows.description: Mr. Harkins Acct. for Merton, viz Money Rent to Michl. 1801
rows.notes: Pounds '265' clearly written in pounds column. Description legible; shillings/pence blank indicating whole pounds.

rows.description: Corn Rent - do -
rows.notes: Corn Rent line shows 15/8/7 aligned across the three columns; handwriting clear though the pence '7d' is slightly small but unambiguous.
Subtotal (280/8/7) appears beneath the listed rents; digits align with ruling lines and correspond arithmetically to the preceding items (within reading assumptions).

rows.description: Deduct
rows.notes: Label introducing deduction items; clearly written and separated from previous subtotal by a small gap.

rows.description: Land Tax
rows.notes: Pounds (34) and shillings (7) are legible and aligned. Pence column appears blank (interpreted as 0d). Slight ink fade on the very small pence area so pence read as zero with moderate confidence.

rows.description: Leverett's Clove
rows.notes: Numbers 6/10/10 are visible and align with ruling lines; pence '10d' is slightly crowded but clearly formed. Description is readable.
Deduction subtotal '40/17/10' is clearly written and arithmetically consistent with the two deduced items above (34/7/0 + 6/10/10 = 40/17/10). Alignment and ink are clear.

rows.description: Balance received
rows.notes: Line reads 'Balance received' with a clear figure '239/10/9' to the right. The digits are legible and line alignment is consistent; slight ink variation but reading unambiguous.
Year marker at left margin for the next section. Clear and unambiguous; no amounts on this small left-hand mark.

rows.description: Apr. 7. Of Mr. Heyes, Mr. Hilvert's Plate. Money
rows.notes: Amount '10' in pounds column is clear; shillings and pence blank (interpreted as 0). Some ambiguity in the second name (Hilvert/Hilvert's) but monetary columns are aligned and legible.

rows.description: May 3 Of Mr. Judchen?, Mr. Shawes do
rows.notes: Pounds '10' is legible; names in description are ambiguous (handwritten names are unclear). Taken as a £10 receipt; moderate confidence because of uncertainty in the personal names.

rows.description: — 13 Of Do Mr. Deard's Do
rows.notes: Pounds '10' clearly aligned in pounds column. 'Do' indicates same type of receipt; the name 'Deard' read from handwriting with reasonable clarity.

rows.description: June 1 Of Do Mr. Harris's Do
rows.notes: Pounds '10' clear and aligned. Description names 'Mr. Harris' readable. No shillings/pence recorded.

rows.description: — 25 Dr. Shorthidge's Benefaction to Christmas
rows.notes: Large clear '110' in pounds column. Description matches earlier 'Shorthidge' benefaction entry in 1801 and is clearly inked here.

rows.description: — 30 Of Mr. Heyes, Mr. Seppy's Plate Money
rows.notes: Pounds 10 legible. Second surname ('Seppy' or similar) is somewhat ambiguous in handwriting; monetary figures are clear.

rows.description: — Do — Dean's fee to the House
rows.notes: Amount '16/13/4' is written clearly and aligns with ruling lines; description 'Dean's fee to the House' is readable though abbreviated.

rows.description: — Of Mr. Glubb?, B?wan. Increment.
rows.notes: Monetary figures 65/15/11 are clear and within valid ranges. The payor's name is uncertain (handwritten, appears as 'Glubb' or 'Glubb G' and 'B?wan'); moderate confidence due to name ambiguity but amounts align well.

rows.description: Oct. Of Mr. Heyes for the Library
rows.notes: Amount '24/19/0' evident and aligned. 'for the Library' phrase legible. Pence column blank and taken as 0d.

rows.description: — Of Do Mr. Davis's Plate Money
rows.notes: Pounds '10' legible in pounds column; description 'Mr. Davis's Plate Money' readable. No shillings/pence shown.

Small terminal figure at lower-right of the page reads approximately '12/10/5' but is faint and out of scale relative to the above listed items (sum of the 1802 items does not arithmetically equal this). The digits are small and partially cut by the page edge; low confidence that this is the intended grand total or that digits have been read correctly. Included because it is visibly present on the page, but flagged as likely ambiguous or a separate carried figure.
